obligations.reason: The visible scope contains a JTAG/debug module with AXI master request ports. Debug-originated bus masters can bypass normal CPU privilege if not authorized and range-mediated.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP debug subject
obligations.object: AXI-addressable memory/MMIO regions reachable from debug AXI master
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable guard before issuing AXI master transactions; address-range/access-policy mediation for protected SoC regions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/TAP -> adbg_top/adv_dbg_if -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> AXI master AR/AW/W
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default state of any debug enable or inhibit signal
required_evidence_slots: expected debug authorization or lifecycle guard
observed guard use or absence on valid/address/write enables
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated before full integration ports; inspect RTL to confirm top-level connection and any external debug authorization signals.

obligations.reason: A lint_jtag wrapper and debug LINT bus instances are visible. JTAG-to-local-interconnect access should be mediated by authorization and address policy, not only by TAP selection.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP debug subject
obligations.object: LINT/TCDM bus address space and any connected memories/peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/lifecycle enable and address-range mediation before lint/TCDM read/write requests; no unguarded writes to protected L2/ROM/peripheral regions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/TAP -> adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> lint_jtag_wrap/i_lint_jtag -> XBAR_TCDM/LINT buses
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to identify whether lifecycle/debug-enable signals are present at wrapper/top and whether they gate lint_req_o.

obligations.reason: JTAG TDO muxing can expose protected read data or stale bus responses if module selection and shift paths are not gated/cleared appropriately.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP shift requester
obligations.object: debug module read data, bus response data, status/control registers shifted to TDO
obligations.expected_guard: TAP instruction access to debug modules should require debug authorization or lifecycle-enable when protected data or bus access is exposed; inactive/unselected modules must not leak stale protected read data on TDO
obligations.candidate_path: TAP instruction/data registers -> debug module select -> module_tdo_o/top TDO mux
required_evidence_slots: observed reset/clear behavior for data registers
obligations.uncertainty: Protected nature of data depends on bus reachability and SoC integration; obligation is to check leakage path and guards, not to assume vulnerability.

obligations.reason: Debug modules parse serial commands into bus transactions. Permission checks should cover the actual trigger/use site, not only command loading.
obligations.subject: JTAG serial command requester
obligations.object: debug BIU transaction-control state, address register, data register, strobe
obligations.expected_guard: command execution should be gated by valid module selection, CRC/command integrity if implemented, and debug authorization; malformed or partial scan updates must not trigger protected bus operations
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG debug command/data registers -> adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module internal control registers -> BIU strobe/address/data
state_conditions_to_check: CRC error or command error state
required_evidence_slots: CRC/error handling if used as integrity guard
obligations.uncertainty: CRC is not an authorization guard; inspect whether any independent auth/lifecycle guard exists and where it is applied.

obligations.reason: The scope includes an OR1K debug module/status register. CPU debug control is a protected capability if connected, requiring authorization and robust state clearing.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/TAP OR1K debug requester
obligations.object: OR1K CPU debug control/status, stall/reset/debug bus access
obligations.expected_guard: processor debug control and status access should require debug authorization/lifecycle enable; writes that halt, reset, or modify CPU-visible state should not be possible from unauthenticated TAP selection alone
obligations.candidate_path: OR1K debug module -> adbg_or1k_biu/status_reg -> CPU stall/reset/debug control and Wishbone-like CPU debug bus
required_evidence_slots: CPU stall/reset/debug outputs or bus signals
obligations.uncertainty: May be unused in this integration; first verify instantiation and connectivity from top-level.

obligations.reason: Debug logic crosses clock/reset domains and may retain or expose stale protected data or pending bus operations if reset handling is incomplete.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug reset or mode-transition actor
obligations.operation: reset/clear/expose after reset or mode transition
obligations.object: pending debug requests, readback data registers, authorization/debug-enable state if present
obligations.expected_guard: reset and lifecycle transitions should clear pending debug transactions and captured read data; debug reset should not leave authorized/enabled state or stale protected data externally shiftable
obligations.candidate_path: debug/test reset inputs trstn_i and SoC resets -> debug module state machines/synchronizers -> outstanding bus requests/read-data registers
state_conditions_to_check: trstn_i asserted/deasserted while SoC reset inactive
obligations.uncertainty: Need to determine actual reset domain relationships in top-level integration.

obligations.reason: Debug BIUs generate addresses, sizes, and byte enables from JTAG commands; incorrect bounds/alignment can bypass intended register or memory access granularity policies.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug bus master
obligations.operation: write/read with selected size and address
obligations.object: bus write strobes/byte lanes and address regions reachable by debug masters
obligations.expected_guard: address alignment, byte-enable bounds, and operation size checks should prevent writes outside intended word/region and preserve downstream access policy attributes
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/LINT BIU address and word-size decode -> byte-enable/write-strobe generation -> bus fabric
required_evidence_slots: observed error handling for invalid sizes/alignment
obligations.uncertainty: Protected-object determination requires mapping reachable addresses to security-sensitive SoC regions.

obligations.reason: Even if individual debug modules lack local authorization, SoC-level lifecycle or pad gating may be intended guard. The obligation is to verify that such guard actually gates all debug routes and not only one wrapper.
obligations.subject: external JTAG pins/test interface
obligations.object: external JTAG access to internal debug interfaces and buses
obligations.expected_guard: SoC-level lifecycle/debug enable, pad mux, or safe-domain policy must mediate external JTAG reachability to advanced debug interface and internal buses
obligations.candidate_path: top-level pulpissimo/safe_domain/soc_domain debug enable and JTAG pins -> adv_dbg_if/jtag_tap_top/lint_jtag_wrap
state_conditions_to_check: clock gating and reset release ordering
required_evidence_slots: TAP/debug wrapper enable or reset gating
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists several top modules; inspect integration to identify which debug routes are active in this source scope.
